California
SUPERSEDED BY 1998
F
r Phi I-
4b
1
AMENDMENTS TO
‘-‘
REGULATION 18530
Practices Commission
August 25,
1994
Honorable David Knowles
-
Assemblymember,
Fourth District
State Capitol
Sacramento,
CA
95814
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
A-94-255
Dear Assemblymember Knowles:
This is in response to your letter requesting advice regard
ing the provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the “Act”)
1
regulating mass mailings at public expense.
QUESTION
May public funds be used to produce and mail holiday greeting
cards to your constituents?
CONCLUSION
The Act does not prohibit you from using public funds to
produce and mail holiday greeting cards to your constituents.
However you may not send more than 200 cards in a calendar
month at public expense.
Moreover, Section 85300 of the Act
prohibits the use of public funds for mass mailings that advocate
or promote your election to office.
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations, Sections
18000-18954.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95804-0807 •
(916) 322-5660


Our File No.
A—94-255
Page
2
-
DISCUSSION
Mass Mailings at Public Expense
In June 1988 Section 89001 was amended by Proposition 73 to
provide:
“No newsletter or other mass mailing shall be sent at
public expense.”
Regulation 18901(a)
2 clarifies that a mailing is
prohibited by Section 89001 only if all of the following apply:
(1)
Any item sent is delivered,
by any means,
to the recipient at his or her residence, place of
employment or business,
or post office box.
For
purposes of this subdivision
(a) (1), the item
delivered to the recipient must be a tangible item,
such as a videotape, record,
or button,
or a
written document.
(2)
The item sent either:
(A)
Features an elected officer
affiliated with the agency which produces or
sends the mailing,
or
(B)
Includes the name, office,
photograph,
or other reference to an elected
officer affiliated with the agency which
produces or sends the mailing,
and is prepared
or sent in cooperation, consultation,
coordination,
or concert with the elected
officer;
(3) (A)
Any of the costs of distribution is
paid for with public moneys; or
(B)
Costs of design, production,
and
printing exceeding $50.00 are paid with public
moneys,
and the design, production, or
printing is done with the intent of sending
the item, other than as permitted by this
regulation.
(4)
More than two hundred substantially
similar items are sent,
in a single calendar month,
excluding any item sent in response to an
unsolicited request and any item described in
subdivision (b);
Regulation 18901(a).
2
The Commission’s authority to interpret Section 89001 to avoid
the absurd results of its literal application was upheld in Watson
v. Fair Political Practices Corn.
(1990)
217 Cal.App.3d 1059.


Our File No. A-94-255
Page
3
Pursuant to Regulation 18901, if the cards will contain your
name, reference to your office, or other reference to you, the
mailing of the cards would be subject to Regulation 18901 and no
more than 200 could be sent.
3
Use of Public Funds
Section 85300 of the Act prohibits the use of public funds
for the purpose of seeking elective office.
This section would
not apply to the holiday cards so long as they are not used to
advocate or promote your election to office.
However,
you should
contact the Legislative Counsel to determine whether any other
laws or any rules of the Legislature would prohibit such an
expenditure.
If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contactme at
(916)
322—590l.
Sincerely,
Steven G.
Churchwell
General Counsel
-
By:
John W. Wallace
Counsel,
Legal Division
As I discussed with Mr. Hastings,
you may use campaign funds
for this purpose and not be subject to the restrictions in
Regulation 18901.
Moreover, if campaign funds are used, it is
Commission policy to prmit the mailing of the holiday greeting
cards without sender identification otherwise required by Section
84305.
(Brown Advice Letter,
No. A-83--296.)
Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries.
Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of
1OQ per page.

18000-18954.

6 of the California Code of Regulations.
